caption:
What if the Hanuman Chalisa was a comic your child couldn't put down?

40 verses. 40 illustrated pages.
Three panels per verse — the story, the scripture, the feeling.
Devanagari, Telugu, and English on every page.

We've been reciting the Chalisa for generations.
Now your child can see it.

📖 Available on Pothi.com → link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

Why did I make this book?

Because I once read the Hanuman Chalisa in a book that had pictures alongside the words.
And it stayed with me far longer than words alone ever had.

This book is that memory — turned into something your child can hold.

Every parent who recites the Chalisa knows this problem:
the child sits there, not understanding, waiting for it to end.

What if instead — they leaned in?

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

श्री गुरु चरण सरोज रज, निज मनु मुकुरु सुधारि।
बरनउँ रघुबर बिमल जसु, जो दायकु फल चारि॥

"With the dust of my Guru's lotus feet, I cleanse the mirror of my mind.
Then I sing the pure glory of Ram."

Before the Chalisa begins, Tulsidas stops.

He doesn't start with power or poetry.
He starts with humility — I am a fool. My mind is clouded.
With my teacher's grace, I will try.

500 years later, we are still reading what that humble man wrote.

This is the lesson before the first verse even starts:
begin with honesty about your limits.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

जय हनुमान ज्ञान गुण सागर,
जय कपीश तिहुँ लोक उजागर।

"Victory to Hanuman — ocean of wisdom and virtue.
Victory to the lord of monkeys, whose light shines across all three worlds."

The very first verse sets the tone for everything that follows.

Before Hanuman's strength is mentioned — his wisdom is praised.
Before his battles — his virtue.

ज्ञान (knowledge) + गुण (virtue) = the foundation.
Strength comes later. But these come first.

This is what the Chalisa teaches our children in the very first breath:
greatness begins with what you know and who you are — not what you can do.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

राम दूत अतुलित बलधामा,
अंजनि-पुत्र पवनसुत नामा।

"Ram's messenger, of incomparable strength.
Son of Anjani, born of the wind."

Hanuman has many names — each one is a story.

Anjani-Putra: son of Anjani, a devoted apsara who prayed for years for a son.
Pavan-Suta: son of the wind god, Vayu — where his speed and strength come from.
Ram Doot: Ram's messenger — the identity he chose above all others.

He could have gone by strength. He chose to go by service.

Our children's names carry meaning too. Do they know what theirs means?

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

महाबीर बिक्रम बजरंगी,
कुमति निवार सुमति के संगी।

"Great hero, valiant, with limbs of lightning.
Remover of evil thoughts — companion of the virtuous."

Bajrangi: body strong as Vajra, the thunderbolt.

But the second line is more powerful than the first.
Hanuman removes kuMati (bad thinking) and stays close to suMati (good thinking).

In a world that pulls children's minds in every direction —
this verse is a prayer for the mind, not just the body.

Stay close to what is good.
Distance yourself from what corrupts.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

कंचन बरन बिराज सुबेसा,
कानन कुण्डल कुंचित केशा।

"Golden complexion, beautifully adorned.
Earrings in ears, curly hair."

Tulsidas paints a picture: golden-skinned, wearing earrings, curly-haired Hanuman.

Not a fearsome warrior here — a radiant, luminous being.

When children only see Hanuman as a roaring fighter, they miss this.
Devotion made him radiant. Strength and beauty coexist in the same form.

Your child doesn't have to choose between power and grace.
Hanuman shows that both are possible.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

हाथ बज्र औ ध्वजा बिराजै,
काँधे मूँज जनेऊ साजै।

"In his hands: the thunderbolt and the victory flag.
On his shoulder: the sacred thread of learning."

Two symbols of power — and one symbol of humility.

The thunderbolt for strength. The flag for victory.
But also the janeu — the sacred thread of a devoted student.

Hanuman is both warrior and student.
Powerful in action, humble in spirit.

He never stopped learning, even after he was already great.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

शंकर सुवन केसरी नंदन,
तेज प्रताप महा जग वंदन।

"Born of Shankara, son of Kesari.
Your radiance and glory are worshipped throughout the world."

Hanuman had two fathers.

Shankar-Suvan: Shiva himself incarnated as Hanuman, to serve Ram.
Kesari Nandan: son of Kesari, the earthly monkey king who raised him.

Divine origin. Humble upbringing. And still — no pride. Only devotion.

What our children are born into matters less than who they choose to become.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

विद्यावान गुनी अति चातुर,
राम काज करिबे को आतुर।

"Learned, virtuous, extraordinarily clever.
Always eager to do Ram's work."

All of Hanuman's qualities — knowledge, virtue, cleverness —
exist for one purpose: to serve.

He is aatur — eager. Not eager for recognition. Not for reward.
Eager for the work itself.

The question for our children:
What are you eager for?

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

प्रभु चरित्र सुनिबे को रसिया,
राम लखन सीता मन बसिया।

"Lover of hearing the Lord's stories.
Ram, Lakshmana, and Sita live in his heart."

Rasiya — one who deeply savors, who is moved by stories.

Hanuman doesn't just know about Ram. Ram lives inside his heart.

This is exactly why this book exists.
When a child reads a story — really reads it, sees it illustrated —
it moves from the page into the heart.

That's what we're trying to do.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

सूक्ष्म रूप धरि सियहिं दिखावा,
बिकट रूप धरि लंक जरावा।

"In a tiny form, he appeared before Sita.
In a fierce form, he set Lanka on fire."

Same Hanuman. Same mission. Two completely different forms.

Before Sita in Ashok Vatika — small and gentle, so as not to frighten her.
Before Ravana's Lanka — enormous and terrifying.

This is true intelligence: knowing which form the moment requires.
Not every situation needs your full power.
Not every situation needs gentleness.

Knowing the difference — that is wisdom.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

भीम रूप धरि असुर संहारे,
रामचंद्र के काज संवारे।

"Taking a fearsome form, he destroyed the demons.
Completing every task of Ramachandra."

Every battle Hanuman fought. Every demon he defeated.
Not for himself. For Ram's mission.

He had no personal agenda. No score to settle. No glory to chase.

This is perhaps the hardest lesson to teach a child:
to be powerful — and use that power entirely for others.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

लाय सजीवन लखन जियाये,
श्री रघुबीर हरषि उर लाये।

"Brought the Sanjeevani herb and revived Lakshmana.
Raghubir embraced him with joy."

Lakshmana lay dying on the battlefield.
The healer couldn't identify which herb was needed.
Night was ending. Time was running out.

Hanuman didn't say: "I don't know which one."

He uprooted the entire mountain and brought it.

When the solution is unclear — bring everything you have.
When you don't know which answer is right — bring all of them.

Ram's embrace was waiting on the other side.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

रघुपति कीन्ही बहुत बड़ाई,
तुम मम प्रिय भरतहि सम भाई।

"Raghupati praised him greatly.
'You are as dear to me as my brother Bharata.'"

This is Ram speaking. To Hanuman. After Lakshmana was revived.

Ram had four brothers. Bharata was the most devoted —
the one who waited 14 years, governing only as a caretaker,
with Hanuman's sandals placed on the throne.

To be compared to Bharata is the highest praise Ram could give.

The child who serves without needing the throne —
that child becomes irreplaceable.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

सहस बदन तुम्हरो यश गावैं,
अस कहि श्रीपति कण्ठ लगावैं।

"A thousand mouths sing your glory.
Saying this, Ram himself embraced Hanuman."

Ram embraced Hanuman. God held his devotee.

A thousand mouths are not enough to describe what Hanuman did.
And yet — Hanuman never sought the praise.
He only sought the embrace.

The child who serves without expecting applause
is the one who earns something far greater.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

सनकादिक ब्रह्मादि मुनीसा,
नारद सारद सहित अहीसा।

"Sanaka and the sages, Brahma and the great seers.
Narada, Saraswati, even the serpent king —"

Tulsidas lists every great being:
sages, gods, the goddess of knowledge herself.

And says: even they cannot fully describe Hanuman.

If Saraswati — goddess of wisdom and words — cannot completely praise him,
who are we?

And yet — Tulsidas tried. With 40 verses.
And so do we.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

जम कुबेर दिगपाल जहाँ ते,
कबि कोबिद कहि सके कहाँ ते।

"Yama, Kubera, the guardians of all directions.
Even the greatest poets cannot describe you fully."

Yama — the god of death.
Kubera — the god of wealth.
The guardians of all eight directions.

None of them can capture Hanuman in words.

This verse teaches something quiet:
some things are beyond description.
Truly great things — you feel them. You don't just explain them.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

तुम उपकार सुग्रीवहिं कीन्हा,
राम मिलाय राजपद दीन्हा।

"You did a great favor for Sugriva.
You introduced him to Ram — and he got his kingdom back."

Sugriva had lost everything.
His wife. His kingdom. Living in exile, afraid of his own brother.

Hanuman found him there — and brought him to Ram.

One introduction. One act of friendship.
Sugriva got his entire life back.

Sometimes the greatest thing we can do for someone
is simply connect them to the right person.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

तुम्हरो मंत्र बिभीषण माना,
लंकेश्वर भये सब जग जाना।

"Vibhishana followed your counsel.
He became king of Lanka — the whole world knows."

Vibhishana was Ravana's own brother.
But he refused to follow adharma.

He came to Ram's side. Hanuman supported him when others doubted.

The result: Vibhishana became king.

Standing with what is right — even when your own family walks the other way.
That is what Hanuman taught Vibhishana.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

जुग सहस्र जोजन पर भानू,
लील्यो ताहि मधुर फल जानू।

"The sun is thousands of yojanas away.
As a child, you swallowed it — thinking it was a sweet fruit."

Baby Hanuman saw the sun rising on the horizon.
He thought it was a ripe mango.
And he leaped into the sky to eat it.

No fear. Pure wonder. Complete confidence.

This is a child's mind — unaware of limits, reaching for what adults call impossible.

We should never fully grow out of that.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

प्रभु मुद्रिका मेलि मुख माहीं,
जलधि लाँघि गये अचरज नाहीं।

"Holding Ram's ring in your mouth,
you crossed the ocean — no wonder."

Before leaping to Lanka, Hanuman held Ram's ring in his mouth.
A symbol. A reminder. A connection to his Lord.

With Ram's name inside him — crossing an ocean was not a miracle.
It was natural.

The question for our children:
What do they carry inside them when they face their own challenges?

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

दुर्गम काज जगत के जेते,
सुगम अनुग्रह तुम्हरे तेते।

"Every difficult task in this world —
becomes easy with your grace."

This verse is not about magic.

It's about the transformation inside a person
when they truly surrender to something greater than themselves.

The obstacles don't disappear.
The person changes.

And that is enough.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

राम दुआरे तुम रखवारे,
होत न आज्ञा बिनु पैसारे।

"You are the guardian at Ram's door.
No one enters without your permission."

Hanuman stands at the threshold — not to keep people out,
but to make sure they arrive with the right heart.

This is why devotees pray to Hanuman first.
Not because he is greater than Ram.
But because he is the bridge.

Some doors open easier when the right person walks with you.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

सब सुख लहै तुम्हारी शरना,
तुम रक्षक काहू को डरना।

"All happiness comes to those in your shelter.
With you as protector — there is nothing to fear."

A verse for every parent who worries.

काहू को डरना — there is nothing to fear.
Not poverty. Not failure. Not the unknown.

This is not blind faith.
It is the lived experience of millions across five centuries.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

आपन तेज सम्हारो आपै,
तीनों लोक हाँक तें काँपै।

"You alone can contain your own radiance.
All three worlds tremble at your roar."

Hanuman's power is so vast — only he can control it.
When he roars, heaven, earth, and the underworld shake.

But he holds it back.
Deliberately. Quietly.

The most powerful force in the room
is often the one that knows when not to act.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

भूत पिशाच निकट नहिं आवै,
महाबीर जब नाम सुनावै।

"Evil spirits do not come near —
when the name of Mahaveer is spoken."

Generations of mothers have whispered Hanuman's name over sleeping children.

Not superstition.
Trust built across centuries.

Some protection doesn't need explaining.
It just needs remembering.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

नासै रोग हरे सब पीरा,
जपत निरंतर हनुमत बीरा।

"All disease vanishes, all pain is gone —
for those who chant Hanuman's name continuously."

निरंतर — continuously. Always.

Not once at Diwali.
Not only on Tuesdays.
Always.

This is what the Chalisa teaches:
make the name a habit — and the protection becomes constant.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

संकट से हनुमान छुड़ावै,
मन क्रम बचन ध्यान जो लावै।

"Hanuman frees from every trouble —
those who remember him in thought, deed, and word."

Three levels: मन (mind). क्रम (action). बचन (speech).

All three aligned.

Devotion is not just chanting with the mouth.
It must reach the mind and show in actions.

When all three come together — that is when something shifts.

📖 The Hanuman Chalisa Comic — link in bio

#HanumanChalisa #JaiHanuman #HanumanJi #BajrangBali #Tulsidas #HanumanBhakt #HinduParenting #RaisingHinduKids #IndianParenting #TeachYourChild #HinduKids #SpiritualParenting #ComicBook #IllustratedBook #DevotionalArt #IndianComics #MythologyArt #HinduMythology #KidsBooks #HanumanArt

सब पर राम तपस्वी राजा,
तिनके काज सकल तुम साजा।

"Ram, the ascetic king, reigns over all.
And you — you complete every one of his works."

Ram was a king who lived like an ascetic.
No attachment to comfort. No ego of royalty.

And Hanuman completed everything Ram set out to do.

Behind every great mission —
there is someone who does the actual work.
Faithfully. Without seeking the throne.

और मनोरथ जो कोई लावै,
सोई अमित जीवन फल पावै।

"Whatever desire anyone brings before you —
they receive boundless fruit in this life."

This is not a shopping list verse.

It is a promise: come with sincerity, and life becomes abundant.

The fruit (phal) here is not just material.
It is meaning. Purpose. Connection.
The things that make a life feel full.

चारों जुग परताप तुम्हारा,
है परसिद्ध जगत उजियारा।

"Your glory spans all four ages —
celebrated and radiant throughout the world."

Satyug. Tretayug. Dwaparyug. Kalyug.

Hanuman's name has shone across all of them.

We are in Kalyug — the age of noise and confusion.
And still, the Chalisa is recited daily by hundreds of millions.

Some light simply does not fade.

साधु-संत के तुम रखवारे,
असुर निकंदन राम दुलारे।

"Protector of saints and sages.
Destroyer of demons — beloved of Ram."

Protector and destroyer. Both in the same verse.

The same Hanuman who gently carried Sita's message
also destroyed Ravana's army.

Protecting the good and removing what harms —
this is not contradiction.
This is balance.

अष्ट सिद्धि नौ निधि के दाता,
अस बर दीन जानकी माता।

"Giver of the eight siddhis and nine nidhis —
this boon was granted by Mother Sita herself."

After Hanuman found Sita in Lanka and brought Ram's message —
she gave him a blessing.

Eight supernatural powers. Nine forms of wealth.
To give to whoever seeks them.

Sita — who had nothing in that moment, a prisoner in an enemy garden —
still gave.

The greatest gifts come from those who have suffered the most.

राम रसायन तुम्हरे पासा,
सदा रहो रघुपति के दासा।

"You hold the elixir of Ram.
May you always remain Ram's servant."

Rasayan — the ancient elixir of life. The potion of immortality.

Tulsidas says: Hanuman holds Ram's name as that elixir.

And his greatest wish for Hanuman: remain a servant.
Not a king. Not worshipped above Ram.
A servant. Forever.

Greatness that never forgets its source.

तुम्हरे भजन राम को पावै,
जन्म-जन्म के दुख बिसरावै।

"Through your worship, one attains Ram.
Sorrows of birth after birth are forgotten."

Hanuman does not keep you for himself.
His entire purpose is to take you to Ram.

A true guide always points beyond himself.

This is the shortcut the Chalisa offers:
come to Hanuman with devotion —
and he will take you the rest of the way.

अन्त काल रघुबर पुर जाई,
जहाँ जन्म हरि-भक्त कहाई।

"At life's end, one goes to Ram's abode.
And wherever born again — as a devotee of Hari."

Even death is addressed in the Chalisa.

For Hanuman's devotees: they reach Ram's realm.
And if they return to this world — they return as devotees.

The thread of faith continues across lifetimes.

What we pass down to our children
is not just a prayer. It is a thread that can outlast us.

और देवता चित्त न धरई,
हनुमत सेइ सर्व सुख करई।

"One need not contemplate any other deity.
Serving Hanuman alone brings all happiness."

Read it this way:

When your heart is fully given to one —
when devotion is total, not scattered —
that is when it transforms you.

Full devotion, not divided attention.
Complete surrender, not partial effort.

This is the difference between going through the motions
and actually being changed.

संकट कटै मिटै सब पीरा,
जो सुमिरै हनुमत बलबीरा।

"All troubles end, all pain is erased —
for those who remember the mighty Hanuman."

The key word here is sumire — to remember.
Not just to recite. To remember.

There is a difference between a child who recites a prayer
and a child who understands what they're saying.

This book exists to close that gap.

जै जै जै हनुमान गोसाईं,
कृपा करहु गुरुदेव की नाई।

"Victory, victory, victory to Hanuman.
Show your grace as a Guru would."

The only verse in the Chalisa with triple repetition.

Tulsidas is almost out of words.
He can only say: victory, victory, victory.

And then — one final request:
Be my Guru. Not just my protector. My teacher.

The highest thing you can ask of Hanuman.

जो सत बार पाठ कर कोई,
छूटहि बंदि महा सुख होई।

"Whoever recites this 100 times —
all bonds are broken, and great happiness comes."

100 times. Not once for good luck.
Sustained practice. Repeated return.

What we practice 100 times becomes part of us.
What our children practice becomes their foundation.

Not what they were told once. What they returned to — again and again.

जो यह पढ़ै हनुमान चालीसा,
होय सिद्धि साखी गौरीसा।

"Whoever reads this Hanuman Chalisa —
attains perfection. Shiva himself is witness."

Tulsidas calls Shiva as witness.
The universe itself makes this promise:
read this, and something in you will be perfected.

Not perfection of achievement.
Perfection of the heart.

तुलसीदास सदा हरि चेरा,
कीजै नाथ हृदय मँह डेरा।

"Tulsidas is forever Ram's servant.
O Lord — make your home in my heart."

The last verse. The final request.

Not gold. Not power. Not even liberation.

Just this: live in my heart.

For 40 verses, Tulsidas praised Hanuman.
And all he wanted in return — was for Hanuman to stay.

The Chalisa ends where it should: with a quiet, open heart.

पवन तनय संकट हरन, मंगल मूरति रूप।
राम लखन सीता सहित, हृदय बसहु सुर भूप॥

"Son of the Wind, remover of troubles, embodiment of auspiciousness.
Together with Ram, Lakshmana, and Sita — dwell in my heart."

The closing prayer is simple.

After 40 verses, after all the battles and miracles and praises —
this is the ending.

An invitation. A door left open.

Come home to my heart. Bring everyone with you.

—

All 40 verses. Illustrated. Devanagari, Telugu, English.
